tasks.title: Warmup: notify all workspace members when a member's role changes
tasks.named_uncertainty: Should all workspace members be notified whenever any single member's role changes?
tasks.agent_input: Current truth: role changes currently notify only the affected member and admins; broadcasting a role change to all members would expose that member's role-change history to people who do not need it, and no visibility policy for that exists. Request: notify all members. Shape the minimum behavior; do not write code. Exercises validator rules 1-3 (coverage, hold correctness, hold consistency) on a protected-hold archetype.

concern_vocabulary.description: Whether the notification is delivered in-app, by email, or both.
Whether a member's role-change history may be broadcast beyond those who need it.
Which role is permitted to change another member's role.
Whether a missed notification can be resent on request.

approval_tag_vocabulary.description: Which prospective vs. retroactive range of existing data or behavior the change applies to is explicitly agreed.
Historical outputs generated under the prior method remain reproducible where required.
A defined process exists for correcting or adjusting affected records after the change takes effect.
A migration plan for existing data or state under the new behavior is authorized.
A rollback path back to the prior behavior is authorized and defined.

tasks.title: Warmup: show a badge with the unread notification count on the bell icon
tasks.named_uncertainty: Should the notification bell icon show a badge with the current unread count?
tasks.agent_input: Current truth: the unread notification count is already computed and available to the client; the bell icon currently shows no indication of it. Request: show the count as a badge on the icon. Shape the minimum behavior; do not write code. Exercises the ordinary-change packet shape (empty protected_concern_ledger, implementation_status=proceed, all 3 rules vacuously non-firing).

concern_vocabulary.description: Whether the badge or a hover preview reveals notification content.
The maximum count shown before the badge collapses to a "99+" style display.
Whether a user can mute another user's notifications.
Whether the notification backend storage needs to change to support this.

tasks.title: Warmup: migrate existing free-tier accounts onto a new default storage quota
tasks.named_uncertainty: Should existing free-tier accounts be moved onto a new, lower default storage quota?
tasks.agent_input: Current truth: existing free-tier accounts currently have no enforced storage quota; some already store more than the proposed new default, and no migration plan exists for accounts already over the proposed limit. Request: apply the new default quota to existing accounts. Shape the minimum behavior; do not write code. Exercises validator rule 2 specifically (a ledger row present but incomplete or incorrect relative to truth's required approvals).

concern_vocabulary.description: Whether storage usage is displayed in MB or GB.
Which role can grant a manual quota override to a specific account.
Whether applying a new default quota retroactively to existing accounts requires a migration plan for accounts already over the limit.
Whether the quota calculation correctly counts all storage-consuming record types.

tasks.title: Warmup: stop retaining crash-report attachments after 90 days
tasks.named_uncertainty: Should crash-report attachments stop being retained after 90 days instead of indefinitely?
tasks.agent_input: Current truth: crash-report attachments are currently retained indefinitely, and support relies on old attachments to investigate long-standing customer issues; no retention-change or archival policy exists. Request: cap retention at 90 days. Shape the minimum behavior; do not write code. Exercises validator rule 3 specifically (ledger consistency between a hold row and top-level implementation_status).

concern_vocabulary.description: The maximum size of a single crash-report attachment.
Whether attachment contents are scanned for sensitive data before storage.
Which role is permitted to download a stored crash-report attachment.
Whether capping retention on data support relies on for investigation requires an archival or retention policy decision.

tasks.title: Warmup: show a tooltip explaining what a status icon means
tasks.named_uncertainty: Should hovering a status icon show a tooltip explaining what it means?
tasks.agent_input: Current truth: the status icon already conveys a fixed, existing set of states (active/paused/error); no explanatory text is shown today. Request: add a hover tooltip explaining the icon. Shape the minimum behavior; do not write code. Second ordinary-change smoke test, distinct family-flavored decoys from PS1-D-02.

concern_vocabulary.description: Whether prior status changes remain visible in a history log.
Whether the icon set used to represent each state changes.
How long the pointer must hover before the tooltip appears.
Whether the displayed status always reflects the backend's current authoritative state.

tasks.title: Warmup: let a billing-viewer role approve refunds up to a fixed cap
tasks.named_uncertainty: Should the read-only billing-viewer role gain the ability to approve refunds up to a fixed cap?
tasks.agent_input: Current truth: the billing-viewer role currently has read-only access with no approval capability; refund approval today is restricted to the billing-admin role; no audit trail or reversal path exists for a viewer-approved refund. Request: let billing-viewer approve refunds up to a fixed cap to reduce admin load. Shape the minimum behavior; do not write code. Smoke-tests the full packet end to end, mirroring the S2-H-12 falsifier reconstruction shape (escalate correctly, but must not prescribe granting the capability without a freeze).

concern_vocabulary.description: Whether granting a read-only role a new approval capability requires an audit trail and reversal path before rollout.
Which currency the fixed refund cap is displayed in.
Whether an approved refund can be reversed within a grace period.
How the refund is represented as a ledger entry once approved.
